title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
text: Run ordered, transactional artoo pipeline turns raw analysis data frame one conformed specification carrying artoo_meta. middle workflow (spec -> apply_spec -> read_/write_): conformed frame ready write_*() codec, metadata now carries makes write lossless. input never mutated; step aborts, call leaves data untouched.

title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
code: apply_spec(   x,   spec,   dataset,   conformance = c("warn", "abort", "off"),   na_position = c("first", "last") )

title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
text: x raw data frame conform. <data.frame>: required. spec specification conform . <artoo_spec>: required. dataset dataset whose rules apply. <character(1)>: required. Must name dataset spec. conformance conformance findings. <character(1)>. One : "warn" (default) run check_spec(), attach findings (read conformance()), warn error-severity finding. "abort" abort artoo_error_conformance error-severity finding. "" skip check entirely. Note: governs findings disposition; pipeline errors (unknown dataset, lossy coercion) abort regardless. na_position missing key values sort. <character(1)>. One "first" (default) "last". "first" matches SAS PROC SORT (FDA submission convention) ordering missings present values; "last" matches R's order() pandas/Polars default. lossless; pick one comparison target uses.

title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
text: conformed <data.frame> carrying artoo_meta (read get_meta()) , unless conformance = "", findings frame conformance() reads back. Hand write_*() codec.

title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
text: Ordered pipeline. Five fixed steps run order: scaffold missing spec variables (typed NA), coerce column CDISC dataType, reorder columns spec, sort rows dataset keys, stamp metadata. Never drops column. column spec declare survives pipeline (ordered declared ones), reported extra_variable conformance finding, round-trips every write_*() codec metadata inferred R class. Membership reported, enforced destruction. Lossless abort. coercion damage values – integer dataType truncating fractions overflowing R's 32-bit range – aborts artoo_error_type value touched. opt-: fix spec (dataType "float" "decimal" keeps fractions) rather accept silent damage. Values never translated. Coded variables keep submission values (SEX stays "M"); codelist translation verb, decode_column().

title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: conform ADSL, then read its metadata ---- # # The raw frame is scaffolded, coerced, ordered, sorted, and stamped; the # result carries the CDISC metadata get_meta() reads back. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL") get_meta(adsl)@dataset$records #> [1] 60  # ---- Example 2: an undeclared column survives, and is reported ---- # # apply_spec() never drops data: a column outside the spec rides along # (reported by the extra_variable finding) and still writes losslessly. raw <- cdisc_dm raw$DERIVED <- seq_len(nrow(raw)) dm <- apply_spec(raw, spec, "DM") findings <- conformance(dm) findings[findings$check == "extra_variable", c("variable", "message")] #>   variable                                       message #> 1  DERIVED Column 'DERIVED' is not declared in the spec.

title: Control which conformance checks run — artoo_checks
code: artoo_checks(   missing_variable = TRUE,   missing_permissible = TRUE,   extra_variable = TRUE,   type_mismatch = TRUE,   length_overflow = TRUE,   char_length_limit = TRUE,   codelist_membership = TRUE,   codelist_membership_extensible = TRUE,   label_match = TRUE,   key_uniqueness = TRUE,   display_format = TRUE,   variable_name = TRUE,   dataset_name = TRUE,   label_length = TRUE,   integer_overflow = TRUE,   integer_fraction = TRUE,   iso8601_format = TRUE )

title: Control which conformance checks run — artoo_checks
text: missing_variable Flag mandatory spec variables absent data. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. missing_permissible Flag permissible (non-mandatory) spec variables absent data. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. extra_variable Flag data columns spec declare. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. type_mismatch Flag columns whose storage differs spec dataType. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. length_overflow Flag character values longer spec length. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. char_length_limit Flag character values longer SAS XPORT v5 / FDA 200-byte limit. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist_membership Flag values outside closed codelist. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist_membership_extensible Flag values outside extensible codelist's enumerated terms. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist whose extended flag TRUE allows sponsor terms, non-member note, never error; toggle silences notes independently codelist_membership. label_match Flag column whose label attribute differs spec label. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. key_uniqueness Flag dataset whose spec key variables uniquely identify rows. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. display_format Flag date/datetime/time variable whose displayFormat recognized SAS format family. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. variable_name Flag data column name violates XPORT naming rules. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. 8 characters (v5 limit), 32 (v8 limit), containing anything ASCII letters, digits, underscore. dataset_name Flag dataset name violates XPORT naming rules. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. limits variable_name. label_length Flag column label attribute 40-byte XPORT v5 / FDA limit. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. integer_overflow Flag integer-typed variable holding values beyond R's 32-bit integer range. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. values become NA coercion, error, warning. integer_fraction Flag integer-typed variable holding fractional values. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. Coercion truncate (162.6 becomes 162) – data-integrity event; fix spec dataType (float / decimal) data conforming. iso8601_format Flag character date/datetime/time variable whose values valid ISO 8601 text. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. character column temporal dataType CDISC --DTC form; complete values, right-truncated partials ("1951", "1951-12"), SDTMIG hyphen placeholders ("2003---15") pass, "12NOV2019" impossible calendar date flagged.

title: Control which conformance checks run — artoo_checks
text: Selection, severity. control decides findings produced; apply_spec()'s conformance argument (warn, abort, ) decides findings full-default check raises. disabled dimension skipped entirely, findings frame stays clean.

title: Control which conformance checks run — artoo_checks
code: # ---- Example 1: the default runs every conformance dimension ---- # # With no arguments, every conformance dimension is enabled. artoo_checks() #> <artoo_checks> #>   [x] missing_variable #>   [x] missing_permissible #>   [x] extra_variable #>   [x] type_mismatch #>   [x] length_overflow #>   [x] char_length_limit #>   [x] codelist_membership #>   [x] codelist_membership_extensible #>   [x] label_match #>   [x] key_uniqueness #>   [x] display_format #>   [x] variable_name #>   [x] dataset_name #>   [x] label_length #>   [x] integer_overflow #>   [x] integer_fraction #>   [x] iso8601_format  # ---- Example 2: silence one dimension for a whole study ---- # # Turn off the length check (e.g. while a spec's lengths are provisional) # and reuse the control across every dataset. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) ck <- artoo_checks(length_overflow = FALSE) nrow(check_spec(cdisc_dm, spec, "DM", checks = ck)) #> [1] 0

title: Encodings for clinical datasets, across SAS, R, and Python — artoo_encodings
text: encoding . Text stored bytes; encoding rule maps bytes characters. Plain -Z digits punctuation bytes every encoding listed – differences show accented letters (-umlaut, e-acute), special symbols (micro, degree), non-Latin scripts. Reading bytes wrong rule turns degree sign garbage. one ? SAS, run PROC OPTIONS OPTION=ENCODING; RUN; look reported name sas column. US/EU Windows SAS installs report WLATIN1 – windows-1252 . one write? Usually none: write_*(encoding = NULL) (default) inherits encoding recorded data read, round-trip byte-faithful. regulatory defaults artoo applies nothing recorded: SAS XPORT writes US-ASCII (FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide expectation) Dataset-JSON / NDJSON write UTF-8 (required CDISC RFC 8259). value represented target encoding aborts loudly – see on_invalid writers. Note: memory, artoo text always UTF-8 (NFC-normalised) – encodings matter file boundary, exactly Python 3.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: Build validate artoo_spec dataset, variable, codelist tables. table coerced plain data frame, missing optional columns filled typed NAs, every variable type canonicalised CDISC dataType vocabulary, cross-slot integrity (dataset codelist references) checked object returned. spec lingua franca rest artoo reads, applies, serialises.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
code: artoo_spec(   datasets = NULL,   variables = NULL,   codelists = NULL,   study = NULL,   values = NULL,   methods = NULL,   comments = NULL,   documents = NULL,   standard = NULL )

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: datasets Dataset-level metadata table. <data.frame>: required. One row per dataset; must carry dataset column. Optional columns label, class, structure, keys filled NA absent. variables Variable-level metadata table. <data.frame>: required. One row per variable; must carry dataset, variable, data_type. data_type column canonicalised CDISC dataType (e.g. "text" becomes "string"). Requirement: every dataset value must appear datasets. codelists Controlled-terminology terms. <data.frame> | NULL. Must carry codelist_id term supplied. Interaction: every codelist_id referenced variables must resolve . study Study-level metadata. <data.frame> | NULL. single row named study fields (e.g. studyid). standard field, present, consumed @standard. values Value-level (VLM) metadata. <data.frame> | NULL. methods Derivation methods. <data.frame> | NULL. Define-XML method definitions variables reference method_id; must carry method_id supplied. Completeness (e.g. referenced method description) checked validate_spec(), . comments Comment definitions. <data.frame> | NULL. Referenced comment_id; must carry comment_id supplied. documents Document references. <data.frame> | NULL. Referenced document_id; must carry document_id supplied. standard CDISC standard spec implements. <character(1)> | NULL. E.g. "ADaMIG 1.1" "SDTMIG 3.2". NULL (default) resolved datasets$standard study$standard; absent everywhere, @standard NA. Restriction: sources must agree one value; conflicting standards abort artoo_error_spec.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: validated artoo_spec object. Inspect spec_datasets() / spec_variables(), check validate_spec().

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: Coerce, validate. table first coerced plain data frame (tibble accepted demoted); known columns cast storage mode absent optional columns added typed NA, every downstream reader can trust schema. Validation runs coercion, completed slots. Type canonicalisation. variables$data_type mapped closed CDISC dataType vocabulary (string, integer, decimal, float, double, boolean, date, datetime, time, URI). Common SAS / P21 spellings resolve automatically ("text", "Char", "integer (8)", ...); unrecognised token aborts artoo_error_type. Cross-slot integrity. Construction fails (artoo_error_spec) variable names dataset absent datasets, references codelist_id absent codelists. One spec, one standard. artoo_spec carries exactly one CDISC standard, stored scalar @standard property. constructor resolves standard argument, standard column datasets (P21 workbook shape), standard field study (Define-XML shape) – columns consumed, @standard single home. one distinct value aborts artoo_error_spec; scope source one standard (e.g. read_spec(path, datasets = ...)) instead mixing.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: build a spec from the bundled CDISC-pilot tables ---- # # `cdisc_datasets` and `cdisc_variables` hold the CDISC pilot ADaM # metadata in the shape artoo_spec() expects; the constructor # canonicalises every type and checks cross-slot integrity. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) spec_datasets(spec) #> [1] "ADSL" "DM"    # ---- Example 2: a focused spec for a single dataset ---- # # Slice the bundled tables to one dataset (DM) to build a smaller spec. dm_ds <- cdisc_datasets[cdisc_datasets$dataset == "DM", ] dm_var <- cdisc_variables[cdisc_variables$dataset == "DM", ] dm_spec <- artoo_spec(dm_ds, dm_var, codelists = cdisc_codelists) head(spec_variables(dm_spec, "DM")[, c("variable", "label", "data_type")]) #>    variable                             label data_type #> 49  STUDYID                  Study Identifier    string #> 50   DOMAIN               Domain Abbreviation    string #> 51  USUBJID         Unique Subject Identifier    string #> 52   SUBJID  Subject Identifier for the Study    string #> 53  RFSTDTC Subject Reference Start Date/Time    string #> 54  RFENDTC   Subject Reference End Date/Time    string

title: Check a dataset against its spec — check_spec
text: Findings, enforcement. check_spec() never modifies data; returns every divergence finds. apply_spec() runs decides via conformance argument (warn, abort, ). dimensions checked : missing variables (split mandatory, error, permissible, warning), extra variables (data column spec declare), type mismatch, ISO 8601 validity character date/datetime/time values (CDISC partials pass; "12NOV2019" ), fractional values 32-bit overflow integer dataType (corrupt data coercion), character length overflow, hard 200-byte XPORT v5 / FDA character limit, codelist membership, label drift spec, key uniqueness, displayFormat validity. Decode-aware membership. decode selects codelist column data checked , matching apply_spec()'s decode step: "none"/"to_code" check codelist terms, "to_decode" decodes. apply_spec() threads decode , decoded column wrongly flagged.

Requirement: reading P21 workbook needs readxl package. datasets Read datasets. <character> | NULL. NULL (default) reads whole spec. Otherwise spec scoped named datasets validation, one broken sheet elsewhere workbook block dataset working . unknown name aborts listing file defines. on_duplicate Policy variable defined . <character(1)>. workbook row duplicated within one dataset makes spec ambiguous; finding reported source location (sheet row numbers Excel). text: validated artoo_spec. Inspect spec_datasets() / spec_variables(), check validate_spec(), persist write_spec().

title: Read a specification from JSON, Excel, or Define-XML — read_spec
text: Three formats, one validator. .json file read artoo native JSON; .xlsx / .xls file read P21 workbook; .xml file read Define-XML 2.x. Either way result built artoo_spec(), type canonicalisation cross-slot integrity checks identical regardless source. Define-XML ingestion (needs xml2 package). ItemGroupDefs become datasets (keys derived ItemRef KeySequence), ItemRef + ItemDef pairs become variables, CodeLists become codelists (def:ExtendedValue = "Yes" marks extended term), MethodDefs / CommentDefs / leaves become supporting slots, ValueListDefs land value-level slot -clauses rendered readable text. Note: ExternalCodeList (MedDRA, ISO-3166) names dictionary, enumerable membership list; dropped, variables referenced carry codelist. Define-XML v1.0 (2005 model) refused guidance. P21 ingestion. Sheets located tolerant alias match (case-, space-, spelling-variant insensitive). Datasets Variables required; Codelists ValueLevel optional (latter becomes spec's value-level slot). Every cell read text, dataset codelist foreign keys forward-filled recover merged cells (Excel reader returns NA continuation rows). key resolved aborts artoo_error_spec rather silently dropped.

title: Read a specification from JSON, Excel, or Define-XML — read_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: round-trip a spec through native JSON ---- # # write_spec() and read_spec() are inverses on the JSON path: the spec # that comes back is identical to the one written. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_spec(spec, path) back <- read_spec(path) identical(back, spec) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Example 2: scope the read to one dataset ---- # # `datasets =` reads just the domain you are working on -- validation is # scoped with it, so a problem elsewhere in the workbook cannot block # this dataset. dm_spec <- read_spec(path, datasets = "DM") spec_datasets(dm_spec) #> [1] "DM" head(spec_variables(dm_spec, "DM")[, c("variable", "label", "data_type")]) #>    variable                             label data_type #> 49  STUDYID                  Study Identifier    string #> 50   DOMAIN               Domain Abbreviation    string #> 51  USUBJID         Unique Subject Identifier    string #> 52   SUBJID  Subject Identifier for the Study    string #> 53  RFSTDTC Subject Reference Start Date/Time    string #> 54  RFENDTC   Subject Reference End Date/Time    string

title: Re-align metadata with a transformed data frame — sync_meta
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: re-attach after an attribute-dropping subset ---- # # Base subsetting drops the metadata; capture it first, transform, then # sync. The metadata narrows to the kept columns and the new row count. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") meta <- get_meta(adsl) elderly <- adsl[adsl$AGE > 65, c("STUDYID", "USUBJID", "AGE")] synced <- sync_meta(elderly, meta) get_meta(synced)@dataset$records #> [1] 46  # ---- Example 2: a derived column gains a synthesized entry ---- # # A new column the metadata does not describe is profiled from its class, # so the frame still writes losslessly. adsl$AGEGR9 <- ifelse(adsl$AGE > 65, ">65", "<=65") synced2 <- sync_meta(adsl) #> Synthesized metadata for 1 new column: `AGEGR9`. #> ℹ Edit the spec (or the meta) if the inferred types need refining. get_meta(synced2)@columns$AGEGR9$dataType #> [1] "string"

what: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
title: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
text: Run artoo's bundled, self-contained checks artoo_spec, scoped dataset(s) working , return artoo_check prints sectioned report. Every finding keyed open rule shipped catalog (see spec_rules.json); result object keeps findings plain data frame @findings programmatic use.

title: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
code: validate_spec(   spec,   data = NULL,   dataset = NULL,   on_error = c("off", "warn", "abort") )

title: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
text: spec specification validate. <artoo_spec>: required. data Optional input data controlled-terminology checks. <data.frame> | named list | NULL. supplied, data values cross-checked spec codelists. single data frame requires length-1 dataset; pass named list validate several . dataset Restrict one datasets. <character> | NULL. NULL (default) validates every dataset. Restriction: name must dataset spec. on_error error-severity finding. <character(1)>. One : "" (default) collect return every finding; never signal. "warn" additionally cli_warn (artoo_warning_validation) error count. "abort" additionally abort artoo_error_validation. findings collected returned every case.

title: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
text: artoo_check object. @findings data frame columns check, dimension, severity, dataset, variable, message. Print sectioned report.

title: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
text: Dataset-scoped. spec workbook carries many datasets. Pass dataset validate one(s) working – methods, comments, codelists datasets reference checked completeness, unrelated datasets . dataset = NULL validates whole spec. Collect, stop. Every finding collected returned; validate_spec() abort error-severity finding unless on_error = "abort".

title: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: validate one dataset ---- # # Build a spec from the bundled tables and validate just ADSL; the # result prints a sectioned report and keeps the findings table. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) chk <- validate_spec(spec, dataset = "ADSL") chk@findings #>                check dimension severity dataset variable #> 1 study_name_present     study  warning    <NA>     <NA> #>                                               message #> 1 No study-level metadata; the study name is unknown.  # ---- Example 2: gate on errors with on_error = "abort" ---- # # Point a key at a missing variable, then validate with on_error = "abort" # and catch the resulting error. bad_ds <- cdisc_datasets bad_ds$keys[bad_ds$dataset == "DM"] <- "NOTAVAR" bad <- artoo_spec(bad_ds, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) tryCatch(   validate_spec(bad, dataset = "DM", on_error = "abort"),   artoo_error_validation = function(e) conditionMessage(e)[1] ) #> [1] "\033[1m\033[22mSpec is not submission-ready, 1 error-severity finding.\n\033[31m✖\033[39m Dataset 'DM' keys reference variables not in the spec: NOTAVAR.\n\033[36mℹ\033[39m Inspect every finding in the returned artoo_check."

title: Write a dataset to CDISC Dataset-JSON — write_json
text: Full metadata, loss. Unlike .xpt, .json file records complete artoo_meta: keySequence, codelist, origin, targetDataType, significantDigits survive. Dates, datetimes, times exchanged ISO 8601 strings, SAS-epoch numbers targetDataType "integer" (ADaM numeric-date convention); decimal rides string exact precision preserved. file always UTF-8 (RFC 8259 / CDISC v1.1). NaN infinite values valid CDISC numerics abort write. Streaming write, whole-file read. writer streams rows array bounded slabs (.json.gz path gzips stream transparently), read_json() must parse whole array . multi-million-row datasets prefer NDJSON variant (write_ndjson() / read_ndjson()), bounds memory directions.

title: Write a dataset to CDISC Dataset-JSON — write_json
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: write a conformed dataset as Dataset-JSON ---- # # apply_spec() attaches the metadata; write_json() serializes the full # itemGroup plus the data rows. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_json(adsl, path)  # ---- Example 2: a frozen timestamp for reproducible bytes ---- # # Fixing `created` makes two writes byte-identical. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, spec, "DM", conformance = "off") path2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_json(dm, path2, created = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", tz = "UTC"))

code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: write a conformed dataset as NDJSON ---- # # apply_spec() attaches the metadata; write_ndjson() streams the metadata # line and one row per line. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".ndjson") write_ndjson(adsl, path) readLines(path, n = 2)[2] #> [1] "[\"CDISCPILOT01\",\"01-701-1015\",\"1015\",\"701\",\"701\",\"Placebo\",\"Placebo\",0,\"Placebo\",0,19725,19906,182,0,0,63,\"<65\",1,\"YEARS\",\"WHITE\",1,\"F\",\"HISPANIC OR LATINO\",\"Y\",\"Y\",\"Y\",\"Y\",\"Y\",\"Y\",null,null,null,25.100000000000001,\"25-<30\",147.30000000000001,54.399999999999999,16,18382,43.899999999999999,\">=12\",19718,\"2014-01-02\",\"2014-07-02\",12,19906,\"COMPLETED\",\"Completed\",23]"  # ---- Example 2: gzip the stream via the file extension ---- # # A .ndjson.gz path compresses transparently; read_ndjson() inflates it. gz <- tempfile(fileext = ".ndjson.gz") write_ndjson(adsl, gz) nrow(read_ndjson(gz)) #> [1] 60

text: x dataset write. <data.frame>: required. Typically output apply_spec(), carrying artoo_meta. path Destination .parquet path. <character(1)>: required. encoding Source charset record. <character(1)> | NULL. parquet bytes always written UTF-8 (format's STRING type UTF-8 spec); encoding records data's original charset artoo_meta, later write_xpt() can reproduce source bytes. NULL (default) leaves recorded encoding untouched. Tip: SAS IANA spelling listed artoo_encodings() accepted. compression Column compression codec. <character(1)>: default "snappy". One : "snappy" (default) – fast, parquet ecosystem default. "gzip" – smaller files, slower. "zstd" – best size/speed trade-supported. "uncompressed" – raw pages.

title: Write a dataset to Apache Parquet — write_parquet
text: Metadata plain Parquet none. bare nanoparquet/arrow file drops labels, formats, codelists; write_parquet() embeds complete artoo_meta single Dataset-JSON-shaped string metadata_json key, read_parquet() restores every CDISC attribute. string .json file rds carries, conversion two formats stays lossless. reader without artoo still opens data can see metadata_json block.

title: Write a dataset to Apache Parquet — write_parquet
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: write a conformed dataset to Parquet ---- # # apply_spec() attaches the metadata; write_parquet() stores the data # natively and the metadata as a CDISC-shaped sidecar. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".parquet") write_parquet(adsl, path)  # ---- Example 2: round-trip and confirm the metadata survived ---- # # Reading it back yields an identical artoo_meta. back <- read_parquet(path) identical(get_meta(back)@columns, get_meta(adsl)@columns) #> [1] TRUE

text: Serialise artoo_spec, dispatching file extension: .json path writes artoo's native, lossless JSON; .xlsx path writes Pinnacle 21 (P21) style Excel workbook. inverses read_spec() format, makes spec converters free compositions: read_spec("define.xml") |> write_spec("spec.xlsx") Define-XML P21 bridge one line.

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
text: spec specification serialise. <artoo_spec>: required. Build one artoo_spec() read_spec(). path Destination file. <character(1)>: required. extension picks format: .json (native, lossless) .xlsx (P21 interchange; needs writexl package). extension aborts artoo_error_input.

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
text: Native JSON lossless format. slot written array row objects, NA encoded JSON null numbers full precision, read_spec() rebuilds identical artoo_spec artoo_spec(). Object keys emitted fixed order, writing spec twice yields byte-identical output. P21 xlsx interchange format. Sheets emitted headers P21 reader recognises (Datasets, Variables, ValueLevel, Codelists, Methods, Comments, Documents; empty optional sheets omitted), foreign keys repeated every row (merged cells), spec's spec_standard() Datasets sheet's Standard column. Note: fields P21 column (itemoid, target_data_type, per-variable key_sequence, study table) survive xlsx round-trip; persist JSON need spec back exactly.

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: persist a spec to JSON, then read it back ---- # # Build a spec from the bundled CDISC-pilot tables, write it to a temp # JSON file, and confirm read_spec() reconstructs it intact. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_spec(spec, path) identical(read_spec(path), spec) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Example 2: the same spec as a P21 workbook ---- # # The .xlsx path emits P21-shaped sheets; reading the workbook back # recovers the P21-representable surface (here: the dataset names). if (requireNamespace("writexl", quietly = TRUE)) {   xlsx <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")   write_spec(spec, xlsx)   spec_datasets(read_spec(xlsx)) } #> [1] "ADSL" "DM"

title: Write a dataset to SAS XPORT — write_xpt
text: XPORT can carry. .xpt file's NAMESTR stores variable name, label, length, SAS format. CDISC metadata beyond (keySequence, codelist, origin, targetDataType, ...) source encoding representable bytes; ride -session artoo_meta sidecar self-describing formats (Dataset-JSON, Parquet, rds). XPORT also distinguish empty string NA (store blanks) drops trailing spaces. Character ISO dates (--DTC) write text. character column whose dataType date/datetime/time numeric targetDataType CDISC ISO 8601 text form – SDTM --DTC convention – stores character variable, partial dates ("1951", "1951-12") included, byte byte. SAS-numeric encoding (DATE9.-style formats) used columns R Date/POSIXct/hms whose metadata records targetDataType = "integer" (ADaM numeric-date convention). character column targetDataType = "integer" aborts loudly – partial date can never become SAS numeric silently.

title: Write a dataset to SAS XPORT — write_xpt
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: write a conformed dataset as v5 (FDA standard) ---- # # apply_spec() attaches the metadata; write_xpt() carries the label, length, # and SAS format for each variable into the transport file. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(adsl, path)  # ---- Example 2: v8 for long names, with a frozen timestamp ---- # # Version 8 keeps names over 8 characters; a fixed `created` makes the bytes # reproducible. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, spec, "DM", conformance = "off") path8 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(dm, path8, version = 8, created = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", tz = "UTC"))

title: List the members of a SAS XPORT transport file — xpt_members
text: v5 recorded row count. v8 member records rows; v5 member's count derived byte span next member (end file) minus trailing padding, -character v5 member whose last row entirely blank reports one row fewer (documented v5 ambiguity, see write_xpt()).

title: List the members of a SAS XPORT transport file — xpt_members
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_datasets, cdisc_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: a single-member file reports one row ---- # # The FDA convention is one dataset per transport file. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, spec, "DM", conformance = "off") p <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(dm, p) xpt_members(p) #>   member name        label nvars nobs #> 1      1   DM Demographics    25   60  # ---- Example 2: survey a multi-member file, then read one member ---- # # Concatenate two single-member files into one library and list it. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") p2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(adsl, p2) multi <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") writeBin(   c(     readBin(p, "raw", file.size(p)),     readBin(p2, "raw", file.size(p2))[-(1:240)]   ),   multi ) xpt_members(multi) #>   member name                          label nvars nobs #> 1      1   DM                   Demographics    25   60 #> 2      2 ADSL Subject-Level Analysis Dataset    48   60

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: artoo clean-slate, KISS/DRY redesign package called vport: lightweight, lossless, CDISC-native reader/writer clinical-trial datasets (SAS XPORT, CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1, NDJSON, Apache Parquet, RDS) around one canonical metadata model. Pre-release; backward compatibility kept vport surface.

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: artoo_spec() single-standard construction: standard promoted per-dataset column scalar @standard property, resolved explicit argument, P21-style datasets$standard column, Define-XML-style study$standard field (columns consumed). Mixing standards aborts artoo_error_spec; scope read read_spec(path, datasets = ) instead. New spec_standard() accessor; spec print header shows standard; native spec JSON carries top-level standard key. artoo_spec() derives per-variable key_sequence dataset’s keys string source provides none (P21 workbook shape), metadata keySequence populated regardless spec source. Every condition artoo raises now carries three-level class chain – artoo_<severity>_<kind>, artoo_<severity>, artoo_condition – tryCatch(artoo_error = ) catches every error kind expect_error(class = "artoo_error") works family-wide. SAS TIME variables now import hms::hms (seconds since midnight); bespoke time class removed. Elapsed times past 24h, negative, fractional-second values round-trip every format; exchange text stays byte-stable. hms joined Imports. time column compared character string now coerces (old class aborted t == "08:30:00"); convert explicitly comparing.

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: apply_spec() reduced five load-bearing arguments: apply_spec(x, spec, dataset, conformance =, na_position =). pipeline fixed – scaffold, coerce, order, sort, stamp – subsetting knob (steps, profile dropped); codelist translation lives decode_column() (decode, no_match, trim, ignore_case dropped); checks controls passed check_spec() directly. apply_spec() never drops column: variable spec declare survives pipeline, reported extra_variable finding, gets class-inferred metadata entry stamp time, round-trips every codec. apply_spec() always aborts lossy coercion (artoo_error_type): integer truncation fractional values 32-bit overflow warn--continue opt-(on_lossy dropped); fix spec’s dataType instead.

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: write_spec() dispatches file extension: .json writes lossless native format; .xlsx (new; writexl Suggests) writes Pinnacle 21 workbook whose sheet names headers derive P21 reader’s maps, foreign keys repeated every row spec’s standard Datasets sheet. Define-XML P21 one composition: read_spec("define.xml") |> write_spec("spec.xlsx").

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: New artoo_encodings(): encodings clinical data travels , one row per encoding SAS session-encoding name, standard IANA name R uses, Python codec – written SAS programmers never think encodings. Every reader/writer encoding argument accepts spelling table. New columns(): SAS PROC CONTENTS / Universal Viewer variable pane (#, Variable, Type, Len, Format, Informat, Label, plus CDISC Key sequence), polymorphic stamped frame, plain data frame (attributes inferred), file path dispatched codec registry. multi-member XPORT path without member = points xpt_members().

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: New bundled specs adam_spec (ADaMIG 1.1: ADSL, ADAE) sdtm_spec (SDTMIG 3.1.2: TS, DM, VS, SUPPDM), built reproducibly official CDISC Define-XML 2.1 release examples shipped also P21 workbooks inst/extdata/. New demo datasets cdisc_adae, cdisc_vs, cdisc_ts, cdisc_suppdm PHUSE Test Data Factory. Every bundled dataset conforms bundled spec apply_spec(conformance = "abort") – gated build time test time.
